title: Low-cost Testing of 2.4GHz Receiver on Standard Digital ATE: Practical Implementation
authors: T. Vayssade, K. Tahraoui, F. Lefèvre, L. Latorre, F. Azaïs
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: In this paper, we present the implementation of a low-cost solution for the test of 2.4GHz receivers on a standard Automatic test Equipment (ATE). The approach relies on the use of a customized binary signal generated by a digital tester channel as the test stimulus. This binary signal is specifically defined such that it presents similar spectral characteristics in the frequency band of the Device Under Test (DUT) as a conventional analog test stimulus generated by an RF tester channel. Dedicated scripts are developed that permit (i) the selection of an appropriate sampling frequency for the digital channel to avoid spectral corruption in the DUT frequency band, and (ii) the automatic conversion of the I/Q data computed by the ATE in case of a conventional RF stimulus into a binary sequence to be stored in the vector memory of the digital tester channel. Experimental results are provided that validate the efficiency of the proposed solution, under different communication protocols (ZigBee and Bluetooth Low Energy) and signal strengths.
title_zh: 低成本在标准数字ATE上测试2.4GHz接收器：实际实现
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种在标准自动测试设备（ATE）上低成本测试2.4GHz接收器的实现方案。该方法利用数字测试通道生成的定制二进制信号作为测试激励，该二进制信号经过特别设计，使其在被测器件（DUT）的频段内具有与射频测试通道产生的传统模拟测试激励相似的频谱特性。我们开发了专用脚本，以实现以下功能：（i）选择合适的数字通道采样频率，避免在DUT频段内产生频谱干扰；（ii）将ATE在传统射频激励情况下计算出的I/Q数据自动转换为二进制序列，并存储于数字测试通道的向量存储器中。实验结果验证了该方案在不同通信协议（ZigBee和蓝牙低功耗）及信号强度下的有效性。

title: Formal Modeling and Verification of Block Production BABE Protocol in Polkadot
authors: Muhammad Rashid, Nazir Ahmad Zafar, Waheed Shahzad, Lal Khan, Mohammad Zubair Khan, Ibrahim Aljubayri
abstract: Polkadot is an emerging blockchain application in the cryptocurrency ecosystem, where a large number of participants actively take part to earn rewards. Block production in Polkadot is one of the most critical and technically complex components, which is governed by the Blind Assignment for Blockchain Extension (BABE) protocol. In BABE, validators compete in time slots to produce blocks by following the longest chain rule. To determine the eligibility of a specific validator for a given slot, several sub-operations are performed, including block lottery execution, block authoring, and block building procedures. Even a minor flaw in these processes can adversely affect the correctness of the chain and may compromise the overall blockchain operation. To ensure a reliable and error-free system, rigorous formal verification is essential to mathematically guarantee system correctness and reliability. Existing studies mainly rely on informal testing or limited assertion-based validation, while a comprehensive formal verification of BABE is still missing. To bridge this gap, this work presents a formal verification of the BABE protocol using model checking techniques. For this purpose, an integrated behavioral model of BABE is developed using the Process Meta Language (Promela), in which each core operational aspect of the protocol is modeled in detail. After modeling, key safety and liveness properties, such as fairness, availability, integrity, and consistency, are formally specified using Linear Temporal Logic (LTL). Both the Promela model and the corresponding LTL specifications are provided as input to the model checker, which verifies the properties over all possible executions of the model. Additionally, verification results include an analysis of the explored state space, verification time, and memory consumption. This study contributes toward the formal verification of other major Polkadot protocols by demonstrating the applicability of model checking, specifically using the SPIN model checker.
title_zh: Polkadot 中区块生成协议 BABE 的形式化建模与验证
abstract_zh:
Polkadot 是加密货币生态系统中一个新兴的区块链应用，吸引了大量参与者积极参与以获取奖励。Polkadot 中的区块生成是其最关键且技术最复杂的组成部分之一，由“用于区块链扩展的盲选协议”（Blind Assignment for Blockchain Extension，BABE）进行管理。在 BABE 协议中，验证节点通过遵循最长链规则，在时间槽中竞争生成区块。为了确定特定验证节点在给定时间槽中的出块资格，需要执行多个子操作，包括区块抽签、区块创建和区块构建过程。这些流程中的任何微小缺陷都可能对链的正确性产生不利影响，并可能破坏整个区块链的运行。为确保系统可靠且无错误，必须进行严格的**形式化验证**，以数学方式保证系统的正确性和可靠性。

现有的研究主要依赖非正式测试或基于有限断言的验证方法，而对 BABE 协议的全面形式化验证仍属空白。为填补这一空白，本文采用**模型检测技术**对 BABE 协议进行了形式化验证。为此，我们使用**进程元语言**（Promela）构建了 BABE 的集成行为模型，详细建模了协议的每一项核心操作。建模完成后，利用**线性时序逻辑**（Linear Temporal Logic, LTL）对关键的安全性与活性属性（如公平性、可用性、完整性与一致性）进行了形式化描述。随后，将 Promela 模型和相应的 LTL 规范作为输入提交给模型检测工具，以验证模型在所有可能执行路径下是否满足这些属性。此外，验证结果还包括对探索状态空间规模、验证耗时以及内存消耗的分析。

本研究通过展示模型检测（特别是使用 SPIN 模型检测器）在 BABE 协议验证中的适用性，为 Polkadot 其他主要协议的形式化验证奠定了基础，推动了区块链协议向更高可信度与安全性的方向发展。
